other_observers: Jeannie Mitchell , Aaron Shippe , Cecile Lee; Yes , I was the original finder . Relocated next day by Bill Maynard at Rose Pond , Chico Basin ranch.
specific_location: Chico Basin ranch HQ , Cottonwood grove just south of ranch HQ.
description: Warbler which appeared to be a little larger and more elongated than adjacent Townsend's Warbler .Had a long white supercilium , blackish auriculars , white neck spot , gray back and wings with two white wing bars and a yellow throat extending down to the upper breast . remaining underparts white with black flank streaking. Did not see any yellow on lores so this would make it the albilora subspecies.Behaviors: Initially feeding in Russian Olive , then flew to cottonwood where proceeded to glean for food on trunk of tree like a nuthatch.Call: None heardPlumage: fall adult ?
